House Cocktails

Jai & Sayantani

A curated collection of house favourites

23 cocktails

Bee's Knees
Gin
London dry gin · honey syrup · lemon juice

Ingredients

  • 2 oz London dry gin
  • 1 oz honey syrup
  • 1 oz lemon juice
  • Lemon wedge (garnish)

Method

Pour lemon juice, honey syrup, and gin into a shaker. Crack ice and shake until well chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe glass. Garnish with a skewered lemon wedge.

Honey syrup: combine three parts honey to one part water by volume. Mix until combined and pourable.
Boulevardier
Bourbon / Rye
Bourbon · sweet vermouth · Aperol or Campari

Ingredients

  • 2 oz bourbon
  • 1½ oz sweet vermouth
  • 1½ oz Aperol or Campari
  • Orange peel & maraschino cherry (garnish)

Method

Pour vermouth, Aperol, and bourbon into a mixing glass. Add cracked ice and stir until well chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe. Express the orange peel and garnish with it and a cherry.

Multiply quantities by two-thirds to make a smaller drink.
Daiquiri
Rum
Light rum · lime juice · simple syrup

Ingredients

  • 2 oz light rum
  • 1 oz lime juice
  • 1 oz simple syrup
  • Lime wheel (garnish)

Method

Pour lime juice, simple syrup, and rum into a shaker. Crack ice and shake until well chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe glass. Garnish with a lime wheel.

Just enjoy!
Division Bell
Mezcal / Tequila
Mezcal · Aperol · Maraschino liqueur · lime

Ingredients

  • 1 oz mezcal
  • ¾ oz Aperol
  • ½ oz Maraschino liqueur
  • ¾ oz lime juice
  • Lime round & grapefruit peel (garnish)

Method

Pour liquid ingredients into a shaker. Add ice and shake until chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe. Express the grapefruit peel and garnish with it and a lime round.

Just enjoy!
Enzoni
Gin
Gin · Campari · muddled green grapes · lemon

Ingredients

  • 5 seedless green grapes (plus 3 for garnish)
  • 1 oz dry gin
  • 1 oz Campari
  • ¾ oz lemon juice
  • ½ oz simple syrup

Method

Place grapes in a shaker and muddle. Add liquid ingredients, then ice, and shake until chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe and garnish with spare grapes.

Any grapes should work well in the cocktail.
Espresso Martini
Vodka
Vodka · coffee liqueur · cold brew concentrate

Ingredients

  • ¾ oz vodka
  • ¾ oz coffee liqueur
  • 1 oz cold brew concentrate
  • Lemon peel (expressed & discarded)
  • 3 whole coffee beans (garnish)

Method

Pour cold brew concentrate, coffee liqueur, and vodka into a shaker. Crack ice. Express lemon peel into shaker, then discard.

Shake vigorously to develop the foamy top. Pour into a chilled coupe and garnish with coffee beans.

Cold brew concentrate: 1 lb coarse-ground coffee to 2 gallons water. Rest at room temperature 24 hours, stirring intermittently. Strain through mesh strainer and coffee filter.
Final Ward
Bourbon / Rye
Rye · green Chartreuse · Maraschino · lemon

Ingredients

  • 1 oz rye
  • 1 oz green Chartreuse
  • 1 oz Maraschino liqueur
  • 1 oz lemon juice
  • Lemon round & maraschino cherry (garnish)

Method

Combine liquid ingredients in a shaker. Add ice and shake until chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe and garnish with a lemon round and skewered cherry.

A riff on the Last Word. Genepy le Chamois is a suitable replacement if green Chartreuse is unavailable.
Improved Whiskey Cocktail
Bourbon / Rye
Rye · Peychaud's & Angostura · Maraschino · absinthe rinse

Ingredients

  • 2 oz rye
  • 2 dashes Peychaud's bitters
  • 2 dashes Angostura bitters
  • ¼ oz simple syrup
  • ¼ oz Maraschino liqueur
  • Absinthe (for rinse)
  • Lemon peel (garnish)

Method

Pour liquid ingredients into a mixing glass. Add cracked ice and stir until chilled.

Spritz inside of a chilled Gibraltar glass with absinthe. Strain the drink in. Express lemon peel and garnish.

Cousin to the Sazerac — its complexity led to the creation of the Old Fashioned. The original recipe calls for Boker's bitters.
Jasmine
Gin
London dry gin · dry curaçao · Campari · lemon

Ingredients

  • 1½ oz London dry gin
  • ¼ oz dry curaçao
  • ¼ oz Campari
  • ¾ oz lemon juice
  • Lemon peel (garnish)

Method

Pour liquid ingredients into a shaker. Add ice and shake until chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe. Express lemon peel and garnish with it.

This is an intentionally unbalanced drink. Only recommended for guests who dislike any sweetness in their cocktails.
Last Word
Gin
London dry gin · green Chartreuse · Maraschino · lime

Ingredients

  • 1 oz London dry gin
  • 1 oz green Chartreuse
  • 1 oz Maraschino liqueur
  • 1 oz lime juice
  • Lime round & maraschino cherry (garnish)

Method

Pour liquid ingredients into a shaker. Add ice and shake until chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe and garnish with a lime round and cherry.

Genepy le Chamois can be used as a suitable replacement if green Chartreuse is unavailable.
Left Hand
Bourbon / Rye
Bourbon · sweet vermouth · Campari · chocolate bitters

Ingredients

  • 1½ oz bourbon
  • ¾ oz sweet vermouth
  • ¾ oz Campari
  • 4 dashes chocolate bitters
  • Maraschino cherry (garnish)

Method

Pour bourbon, vermouth, Campari, and bitters into a mixing glass. Add cracked ice and stir until chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe. Garnish with a skewered maraschino cherry.

For a less bitter version, substitute Campari with Aperol. The original recipe uses Bittermen's Xocolatl Mole bitters.
Manhattan
Bourbon / Rye
Rye · sweet vermouth · Angostura bitters

Ingredients

  • 2 oz rye
  • 1 oz sweet vermouth
  • 2 dashes Angostura bitters
  • Orange peel & maraschino cherry (garnish)

Method

Pour vermouth, rye, and bitters into a mixing glass. Add cracked ice and stir until chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe. Express orange peel and garnish with it and a cherry.

The Boulevardier is an elevated and better version of this cocktail.
Margarita
Mezcal / Tequila
Tequila · dry curaçao · lime juice · salt rim

Ingredients

  • 2 oz tequila
  • 1 oz dry curaçao
  • 1 oz lime juice
  • Lime wedge & salt (garnish)

Method

Pour liquid ingredients into a shaker. Add ice and shake until chilled.

Rub the outer rim of a chilled small whiskey glass with lime, then run through kosher salt. Pour in drink and garnish with lime wedge.

Replace tequila with mezcal for an Oaxacan margarita — use only ½ oz dry curaçao and add ½ oz agave syrup.
Naked and Famous
Mezcal / Tequila
Mezcal · yellow Chartreuse · Aperol · lemon

Ingredients

  • 1 oz mezcal
  • 1 oz yellow Chartreuse
  • 1 oz Aperol
  • 1 oz lemon juice

Method

Pour liquid ingredients into a shaker. Add ice and shake until chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe glass.

Yellow Chartreuse can be subbed with ¾ oz green Chartreuse + ¼ oz honey syrup. The Division Bell is a similar but better-balanced cocktail.
Naughty Dog
Rum
Dark rum · coffee liqueur · root beer · cream float

Ingredients

  • 1½ oz dark rum
  • 1 oz coffee liqueur
  • 3 oz root beer
  • Float of heavy cream
  • Ground nutmeg (garnish)

Method

Pour coffee liqueur and rum into a highball or glass mug. Fill with ice.

Pour in root beer. Top with a heavy cream float by pouring cream over a bar spoon onto the ice. Sprinkle with ground nutmeg.

Scale to fit the glass — a large mug may need 1.5× quantities. Try Smith & Cross, Appleton 12, or Denizen 8 for the rum.
Negroni
Gin
Gin · sweet vermouth · Aperol · orange peel

Ingredients

  • 1 oz gin
  • 1 oz sweet vermouth
  • 1 oz Aperol
  • Orange peel (garnish)

Method

Place a large ice cube in a whisky glass. Pour in gin, vermouth, and Aperol. Stir until chilled.

Express the orange peel and garnish.

Traditionally made with Campari rather than Aperol. Aperol is less bitter — factor that into your choice.
Old Fashioned
Bourbon / Rye
Bourbon · Angostura bitters · demerara syrup

Ingredients

  • 2 oz bourbon
  • 2 dashes Angostura bitters
  • ¼ oz demerara syrup
  • Lemon peel & orange peel (garnish)

Method

Place liquid ingredients directly in a whiskey glass. Add a large ice cube and stir to chill.

Express both peels and garnish.

Infinitely customisable — more bitters, rye instead of bourbon, maple syrup instead of demerara, or just one fruit peel.
Paper Plane
Bourbon / Rye
Bourbon · Aperol · Amaro Nonino · lemon

Ingredients

  • 1 oz bourbon
  • 1 oz Aperol
  • 1 oz Amaro Nonino
  • 1 oz lemon juice
  • Lemon peel & maraschino cherry (garnish)

Method

Combine ingredients in a shaker. Add cracked ice and shake until chilled.

Pour into a chilled coupe and garnish with lemon peel and a cherry.

Just enjoy!
Penicillin
Scotch
Blended scotch · lemon · honey syrup · ginger · Islay float

Ingredients

  • 2 oz blended scotch
  • ¾ oz lemon juice
  • ½ oz honey syrup
  • ½ oz ginger syrup
  • ½ oz Islay single malt (float)
  • Candied or sliced ginger (garnish)

Method

Pour blended scotch, lemon juice, honey syrup, and ginger syrup into a shaker. Add cracked ice and shake well.

Add a large ice cube to a whiskey glass. Strain in the cocktail. Float the Islay scotch on top. Garnish with ginger.

Best with Monkey Shoulder (blended) and Laphroaig (Islay float). Ginger syrup: blend 1 cup each sliced fresh ginger, sugar, and boiling water, then strain through cheesecloth.
Sazerac
Bourbon / Rye
Rye · Peychaud's bitters · simple syrup · absinthe rinse

Ingredients

  • 2 oz rye
  • 4 dashes Peychaud's bitters
  • 2 barspoons simple syrup
  • Absinthe (for rinse)
  • Lemon peel (garnish)

Method

Pour simple syrup, bitters, and rye into a mixing glass. Add cracked ice and stir until chilled.

Spritz inside of a chilled whiskey glass with absinthe. Strain in the drink. Express lemon peel and garnish.

The Improved Whiskey Cocktail is a similar but marginally more complex sibling.
Spritz
Misc
Aperol · prosecco · club soda · orange

Ingredients

  • 2 oz Aperol
  • 3 oz prosecco
  • 1 oz club soda
  • Orange slice & peel (garnish)

Method

Fill a wine glass with cracked ice. Press an orange slice firmly against the side of the glass.

Pour Aperol, prosecco, and club soda gently to keep distinct layers. Express the orange peel, then discard.

A template for any spritz — replace Aperol with any suitable apéritif or digestif.
Tom Collins
Gin
Gin · lemon juice · simple syrup · club soda

Ingredients

  • 2 oz gin
  • 1 oz lemon juice
  • ½ oz simple syrup
  • Club soda (to top)
  • Lemon wedge (garnish)

Method

Place ice cubes in a highball. Pour in lemon juice, simple syrup, and gin. Stir briefly.

Top with club soda. Garnish with a lemon wedge.

Elevate to a French 75: shake the base ingredients over ice, pour into a chilled coupe, and top with champagne instead of soda. Garnish with a lemon twist.
Whiskey Sour
Bourbon / Rye
Bourbon · lemon juice · simple syrup · egg white foam

Ingredients

  • 2 oz bourbon
  • 1 oz lemon juice
  • 1 oz simple syrup
  • White of 1 egg
  • 3 dashes Angostura bitters (garnish)

Method

Pour lemon juice, simple syrup, bourbon, and egg white into a shaker. Dry shake until frothy.

Add cracked ice and shake again until well chilled and frothy. Double strain into a chilled coupe.

Add three dashes of Angostura bitters on the foam and use a toothpick to create a design.

Shake very well — the foam is the signature of this drink.
